author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
chāḍi’ anya⋅ abhilāṣa, jñāna⋅ karma⋅ sahavāsa,
ānukūlye kṛṣṇānuśīlana
‘śuddhabhakti’ bali tā’re, bhaktiśāstra⋅suvicāre,
śrī-rūpera siddhānta ⋅vacana

śravaṇa, kīrtana, smṛti, sevārcana, dāsya ,nati,
sakhya, ātmanivedana haya
sādhana⋅bhaktira aṅga, sādhakera jāhe raṅga,
sadā sādhujana⋅saṅgamaya

sādhana⋅bhaktira bale, bhāvarūpā bhakti⋅phale,
tāhā punaḥ premarūpa pāya
preme jīva kṛṣṇa bhaje, kṛṣṇa-bhakti-rase maje,
sei rasa śrī-rūpa śikhāya
en_translation:
Giving up other desires, and association with knowledge and fruitive action, the favorable cultivation of Kṛṣṇa is called pure devotion. This is the conclusive statement of Śrī Rūpa Gosvāmī, after careful consideration of the devotional scriptures.

Hearing, chanting, remembering, serving and worshiping, servitorship, offering obeisances, friendship, and self-surrender are the limbs of devotional practice, in which the practitioner finds joy, always in the association of saintly persons.

By the strength of devotional practice, the fruit is devotion in the stage of spiritual emotion, which again attains the form of divine love. In divine love, the soul worships Kṛṣṇa, becoming immersed in the nectar of devotion to Kṛṣṇa. That nectar Śrī Rūpa teaches.
